paper_title: (Theta, triangle)-free and (even hole, $K_4$)-free graphs. Part 2 : bounds on treewidth
summary: The question of whether (triangle, $S_{1,2,3}$)-free graphs have bounded cliquewidth remains open as of 2026. Bounded cliquewidth has been established for the smaller class (triangle, $S_{1,2,2}$)-free graphs (arXiv:1608.01820) and for (triangle, $H$)-free graphs when $H \in \{P_6, S_{1,1,3}\}$, but the $S_{1,2,3}$ case is explicitly identified as open in the source paper. No follow-up paper resolving this question was found in a targeted web search across arXiv and the broader literature.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch of arXiv:2203.06775 confirms it is about (even hole, diamond, pyramid)-free graphs and bounded treewidth (Theorem 1.4), not about (triangle, S_{1,2,3})-free graphs or cliquewidth. The paper does not cite or address the open question from arXiv:2001.01607 about S_{1,2,3} and cliquewidth. The fuzzy match appears to have linked this paper to the wrong conjecture.

notes: No follow-up resolving this open question was found. The closely related settled cases are: (triangle, S_{1,2,2})-free graphs (bounded cliquewidth, arXiv:1608.01820) and (triangle, P_6)-free and (triangle, S_{1,1,3})-free graphs. The S_{1,2,3} case sits just outside the resolved boundary and is the next open case in the hierarchy. The internal reference arXiv:2203.06775 was not verified to be relevant to this conjecture.
